stem: A automotive testing study measures the fuel consumption (L/100km) for a sample of 30 vehicles. The sample mean is 401.0 L/100km with a sample standard deviation of 55.2 L/100km. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 401.0 L/100km. 95% CI = [380.4, 421.6]. Point estimate: 401.0 L/100km.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
